page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor using the x86_64 architecture, the instance features 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core and dedicated CPU allocation. The processor is equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AES, and SHA_NI, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a balanced performance profile. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% of tested instances, while multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench scores fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput, reaching 507.84 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 69.61 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Multi-threaded decompression and extended instruction tests represent relative performance bottlenecks, scoring in the bottom 25%.

This instance offers a high resource density of 8 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring substantial memory without the need for high-end multi-core compute scaling. The combination of strong single-core performance and average memory bandwidth makes it suitable for database operations, caching layers like Redis, static web serving, and lightweight software compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, driven by an AMD EPYC 7B12 x86_64 processor. The hardware configuration includes 4 physical cores with hyper-threading and dedicated CPU allocation, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The processor supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions, enabling efficient cryptographic and integer operations. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results, though multi-threaded decompression remains a relative bottleneck. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, this instance provides a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and general-purpose applications requiring a larger memory footprint.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor. It delivers strong single-core performance for databases and caching.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B12 CPU, built for databases and caching.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-CPU ratio and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
